title: Inventory satellite wetland-change, bird-population, flyway, and protected-area data sources for migratory flyway analysis
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite wetland-change products (JRC Global Surface Water), bird-population databases (IWC, eBird), flyway boundary data, and Ramsar protected-site data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit flyway-coverage assessment.
inputs: JRC Global Surface Water Explorer documentation
eBird flyway coverage and bias assessment
Published flyway-scale bird population trend studies
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and documented flyway-coverage gaps — particularly IWC monitoring asymmetry between European and Asian/African flyways.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, flyway coverage, temporal consistency, and monitoring-gap documentation.

title: Map satellite-detected wetland change at key stopover sites along at least one major migratory flyway
outcome: A stopover-site wetland-change analysis for at least one major flyway, documenting permanent water loss, seasonal change, and conversion trends at sites with bird-population monitoring data, with explicit resolution and tidal-cycle limitations.
inputs: JRC Global Surface Water monthly observations for target flyway region
Ramsar site boundaries and other key stopover-site locations
Administrative boundary data for regional aggregation
Published intertidal wetland mapping studies for tidal-cycle correction
expected_artifact: validation.md update with wetland-change methodology and site-level results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms JRC data extraction, wetland-change classification methodology, and tidal-cycle handling for intertidal sites, with explicit documentation of resolution limitations for small wetlands.
done_condition: Wetland-change analysis includes methodology, site-level results, resolution limitations, tidal-cycle documentation, and replication status for at least one flyway.

title: Link wetland-change trends to migratory bird population counts at stopover sites with multi-threat attribution
outcome: A bird-population correlation analysis linking wetland-change trends to IWC and eBird population counts for at least one flyway, with explicit documentation of monitoring-coverage gaps, temporal consistency limitations, and multi-threat attribution caveats.
inputs: IWC and eBird population count data for target flyway
Published threat assessments for target flyway species
Flyway boundary and stopover-site dependency data
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with correlation methodology and multi-threat attribution documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms population data extraction, correlation methodology, and multi-threat attribution documentation — particularly that wetland loss is not presented as the sole driver of decline.
done_condition: Correlation analysis includes methodology, monitoring-coverage gaps, temporal consistency assessment, multi-threat attribution caveats, and replication status.

title: Red-team wetland-loss attribution overstatement and conservation-priority misuse risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how wetland-bird correlations could overstate the role of wetland loss relative to other flyway threats, how results could be misused to justify conservation-priority changes without multi-threat context, and how site-level analysis could be misinterpreted at flyway scale.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every wetland-bird attribution claim has multi-threat caveats, monitoring-coverage documentation, and explicit non-use cases. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every wetland-bird attribution claim is either blocked, softened to multi-threat context language with explicit monitoring-gap caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match wetland conservation and flyway management decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the wetland-bird analysis would change any real decision for conservation organizations, Ramsar Convention bodies, or flyway management partnerships without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: Ramsar Convention and flyway partnership context
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for conservation and flyway management audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that wetland-loss attribution diverts attention from other flyway threats or that site-level findings are over-extrapolated to flyway scale. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., East Asian-Australasian Flyway Partnership secretariat), named decision (e.g., stopover-site conservation prioritization), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for flyway-scale population decline attribution without multi-threat context), and replicated field-reality review.
